metadata.name: Blevins Phonology I — Universal Feature Set
metadata.citation: Blevins, J. (2025). Phonology I course notes. Feature table compiled from Blevins (2025) Table 1 & Table 2.

notes.convention: Values: '+' present, '-' absent, '0' inapplicable. Italic features (approximant, delayed_release, front, labiodental, tap, trill) should be used only when absolutely necessary per Blevins (2025). Table 2 features (long, stress, tone, upper_register) are stored outside the per-segment matrices because they are prosodic/autosegmental rather than strictly segmental.
revision_notes: Removed Long, Stress, Tone, and UpperRegister from individual segment matrices and represented them separately as nonsegmental features.
Added segment_structure annotations for contour segments (affricates) and complex segments with simultaneous place specifications.
Changed Approximant to '+' for ʍ and ɦ to better match their glide/sonorant treatment.

Long.note: Removed from per-segment matrices; Blevins treats length as better represented outside the segmental feature matrix.

Stress.note: Removed from per-segment matrices; Blevins treats stress as a property of syllables, not individual segments.

Tone.note: Removed from per-segment matrices; Blevins says tonal features normally are not included in the segmental feature matrix.

UpperRegister.note: Removed from per-segment matrices; Blevins treats register with tone as non-segmental.
